title: genlisea glabra
intro: Genlisea glabra, also known as the corkscrew plant or the sticky trap plant, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the Genlisea genus. Native to the tropical regions of Africa, this plant obtains essential nutrients by capturing and digesting small insects, such as ants and spiders. The leaves and roots of the Genlisea glabra are used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas or infusions, which are used to promote digestion, boost energy, and support immune function.
